subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start the GUI, optionally running an alternative program

options.description: Specify the current working directory for the initially spawned program

Override the default windowing system class. The default is "org.wezfurlong.wezterm". Under X11 and Windows this changes the window class. Under Wayland this changes the app_id. This changes the class
Override the default workspace with the provided name. The default is "default"
Override the position for the initial window launched by this process
If true, do not connect to domains marked as connect_automatically in your wezterm configuration file

If enabled, don't try to ask an existing wezterm GUI instance to start the command.  Instead, always start the GUI in this invocation of wezterm so that you can wait for the command to complete by wai
Print help information

subcommands.name: move-pane-to-new-tab
subcommands.description: Move a pane into a new tab

options.description: Specify the pane that should be moved. The default is to use the current pane based on the environment variable WEZTERM_PANE
Specify the window into which the new tab will be created. If omitted, the window associated with the current pane is used
If creating a new window, override the default workspace name with the provided name.  The default name is "default"
Create tab in a new window, rather than the window currently containing the pane
Print help information

subcommands.name: split-pane
subcommands.description: Split the current pane. Outputs the pane-id for the newly created pane on success

options.description: Specify the pane that should be split. The default is to use the current pane based on the environment variable WEZTERM_PANE
The number of cells that the new split should have. If omitted, 50% of the available space is used
Specify the number of cells that the new split should have, expressed as a percentage of the available space
Specify the current working directory for the initially spawned program
Instead of spawning a new command, move the specified pane into the newly created split
Equivalent to `--right`. If neither this nor any other direction is specified, the default is equivalent to `--bottom`
Split horizontally, with the new pane on the left
Split horizontally, with the new pane on the right
Split vertically, with the new pane on the top
Split vertically, with the new pane on the bottom
Rather than splitting the active pane, split the entire window
Print help information

subcommands.name: spawn
subcommands.description: Spawn a command into a new window or tab. Outputs the pane-id for the newly created pane on success

options.description: Specify the current pane. The default is to use the current pane based on the environment variable WEZTERM_PANE. The pane is used to determine the current domain and window
Specify the window into which to spawn a tab. If omitted, the window associated with the current pane is used. Cannot be used with `--workspace` or `--new-window`
Specify the current working directory for the initially spawned program
When creating a new window, override the default workspace name with the provided name.  The default name is "default"
Spawn into a new window, rather than a new tab
Print help information

subcommands.name: send-text
subcommands.description: Send text to a pane as though it were pasted. If bracketed paste mode is enabled in the pane, then the text will be sent as a bracketed paste

options.description: Specify the target pane. The default is to use the current pane based on the environment variable WEZTERM_PANE
Send the text directly, rather than as a bracketed paste
Print help information

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

options.description: When connecting to a gui instance, if you started the gui with `--class SOMETHING`, you should also pass that same value here in order for the client to find the correct gui instance
Don't automatically start the server
Prefer connecting to a background mux server. The default is to prefer connecting to a running wezterm gui instance
Print help information

subcommands.name: imgcat
subcommands.description: Output an image to the terminal

options.description: Specify the display width; defaults to "auto" which automatically selects an appropriate size.  You may also use an integer value `N` to specify the number of cells, or `Npx` to specify the number of

Specify the display height; defaults to "auto" which automatically selects an appropriate size.  You may also use an integer value `N` to specify the number of cells, or `Npx` to specify the number of
Do not respect the aspect ratio.  The default is to respect the aspect ratio
Print help information

subcommands.description: Advise the terminal of the current working directory by emitting an OSC 7 escape sequence

options.description: Print help information
